title: Bishops
description:
A chess bishop attacks every square that shares a diagonal with it.

Place the maximum number of bishops on an $n \times m$ chessboard in such a way that none of them attack each other.
input: The first line contains two integers $n$ and $m$: the dimensions of the chessboard ($1 \leq n, m \leq 10^5 + 1$).
output: On the first line, print an integer $k$: the maximum possible number of bishops on an $n \times m$ chessboard such that they don't attack each other. On each of the next $k$ lines, print two integers: the coordinates of bishops. The first coordinate should be in the range $[1, n]$, and the second in the range $[1, m]$. If there are several possible answers, print any one of them.
source: Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Summer 2023, Day 4: SPb SU Contest, LVII SPb SU Championship
